text: Feature branches are a key part of Git, allowing developers to work on new features separately from the main code. For example, when starting a task, you create a branch, make changes, and commit them. Later, you merge this branch back into the main branch using a pull request, which lets teammates review your code before it is added. This workflow helps avoid conflicts and keeps the project stable. Merging combines changes from different branches, making version control efficient and collaborative.
